questions.text: Why did the Prophet (SAW) 'frown and turn away'?
questions.answer: Because a blind man (Abdullah ibn Umm Maktum) came to him while he was busy talking to the leaders of the Quraysh.

questions.text: What does Allah say about the blind man in Verse 3?
questions.answer: That he might have been purified or received a reminder that would benefit him.

questions.text: What is the lesson for the Prophet regarding the wealthy leaders who were indifferent to faith?
questions.answer: That he is not responsible if they are not purified; his focus should be on those who come seeking knowledge.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the one who comes 'striving and fearing'?
questions.answer: He is the one the Prophet should not be distracted from.

questions.text: How is the Quran described in Verses 13-16?
questions.answer: In honored sheets, exalted and purified, written by the hands of noble and pious scribes (angels).

questions.text: How is the human being described in Verse 17 regarding his ingratitude?
questions.answer: 'Cursed is man; how ungrateful is he!'.

questions.text: From what did Allah create the human being?
questions.answer: From a sperm-drop.

questions.text: What does Allah do for the human after creating him (Verse 20)?
questions.answer: He makes the path (of life/birth/faith) easy for him.

questions.text: What happens after death according to Verse 21?
questions.answer: Allah causes him to die and be placed in a grave.

questions.text: What is the command to man regarding his food (Verse 24)?
questions.answer: 'Let man look at his food'.

questions.text: What is the process Allah describes to produce food?
questions.answer: Pouring down rain, splitting the earth, and growing grain, grapes, olives, and dates.

questions.text: What is 'Abba'?
questions.answer: Herbage or pasture for animals.

questions.text: What is the 'Deafening Blast' (as-Sakhkhah)?
questions.answer: The blast of the Day of Resurrection.

questions.text: Who will a person flee from on that Day?
questions.answer: From his brother, his mother, his father, his spouse, and his children.

questions.text: Why will a person flee from his closest family on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: Because every person that Day will have a matter to occupy him (his own accountability).

questions.text: How will the faces of the believers look on that Day?
questions.answer: Beaming/radiant, laughing, and rejoicing.

questions.text: How will the faces of the disbelievers look on that Day?
questions.answer: Covered with dust and darkness (Ghabarah).

questions.text: Who are the people with dusty/dark faces?
questions.answer: The disbelievers and the wicked (Fajarah).

questions.text: True or False: Surah 'Abasa teaches that everyone is equal in the sight of Allah based on their heart's intention and quest for truth, not their status.
questions.answer: True.

questions.text: What does 'Safaratan Kiramin Bararah' refer to?
questions.answer: The noble and pious angelic scribes of the revelation.
